problems.name: Binary search on sorted array
problems.description: Given a sorted array and a target value (or comparator), return the index of the target or the insertion point if not found. Optionally find the leftmost or rightmost match.

problems.name: Validate JSON schema (subset)
problems.description: Given a value and a JSON Schema object (supporting type, required, properties, items, minLength, maxLength, minimum, maximum, enum), return { valid, errors }.

problems.name: Top-K nearest neighbors by cosine similarity
problems.description: Given a query embedding (number[]) and a list of { id, embedding } candidates, return the top K candidates sorted by cosine similarity descending. Efficient for lists up to ~5000 items.

problems.name: Zip arrays into array of tuples
problems.description: Given N arrays, return an array of tuples where each tuple contains the elements from each input array at the same index. Stop at the shortest array (or pad with undefined for the longest).

problems.name: Group array of objects by key
problems.description: Given an array of objects and a key name (or key-extractor function), return an object where each key maps to an array of items that share that key value.

problems.name: Find files containing pattern
problems.description: Recursively search a directory for files whose content matches a regex pattern. Return { file, lineNumber, lineContent } for each match. Respect .gitignore by default.

problems.name: Encode/decode URL components
problems.description: Properly encode a string for use in a URL path segment or query parameter value, and decode encoded strings back. Handle the difference between encodeURIComponent and encodeURI.

problems.name: Extract exported names from TypeScript module
problems.description: Given TypeScript source code, return all exported identifiers (functions, classes, constants, types, interfaces). Distinguish value exports from type-only exports.

problems.name: Base64 encode and decode
problems.description: Encode a string or Buffer to base64, and decode a base64 string back to a Buffer. Support both standard and URL-safe base64 variants. Handle padding correctly.

problems.name: Pick keys from object
problems.description: Return a new object containing only the specified keys from the source object. Keys that don't exist in the source are omitted from the result.

problems.name: Detect programming language from file extension
problems.description: Given a filename or file extension, return the canonical language name and its ecosystem (e.g. '.ts' → { language: 'TypeScript', ecosystem: 'node' }). Cover at least 30 common languages.

problems.name: Memoize async function with TTL
problems.description: Wrap an async function so repeated calls with the same arguments return a cached promise. Entries expire after a configurable TTL. Cache key derived from JSON-serialized arguments.

problems.name: S3 put and get with content type
problems.description: Put a string or Buffer to an S3 key with the correct ContentType header. Get the object and return the body as a Buffer or parsed JSON. Handle NoSuchKey gracefully.

problems.name: Deterministic JSON stringify
problems.description: Serialize an object to JSON with keys sorted alphabetically at every level of nesting. Produces the same output for the same data regardless of insertion order.

problems.name: Extract bearer token from Authorization header
problems.description: Given an Authorization header value, extract the bearer token if present. Return null if the scheme is not 'Bearer' or the header is malformed.

problems.name: Generate TypeScript interface from JSON sample
problems.description: Given a JSON value (object, array, or primitive), generate a TypeScript interface or type alias that describes its shape. Handle nested objects, optional fields (from nullable values), and arrays.

problems.name: Flatten nested object
problems.description: Given a deeply nested object, return a flat object where nested keys are joined with a delimiter (default: '.'). Arrays are left as-is by default but can optionally be flattened with index notation.

problems.name: Correlate log lines by request ID
problems.description: Given an array of structured log lines (JSON objects with a request_id field), group them by request_id and return an array of correlated traces sorted by timestamp.

problems.name: Word frequency map
problems.description: Given a string, return a map of each word to its occurrence count. Case-insensitive. Ignore punctuation. Sort by count descending, then alphabetically.

problems.name: DynamoDB transactional write
problems.description: Execute a DynamoDB TransactWriteItems call with multiple Put and Update operations. Handle TransactionCanceledException and return which condition failed.

problems.name: Compute p50/p95/p99 from latency samples
problems.description: Given an array of latency measurements in milliseconds, compute p50, p95, and p99 percentiles. Use a sorting-based approach for accuracy. Optionally compute a histogram with configurable bucket size.

problems.name: Parse multipart/form-data body
problems.description: Parse a multipart/form-data request body (given as a Buffer and boundary string) into an array of parts, each with headers and body.

problems.name: Wrap text at column width
problems.description: Wrap a string so no line exceeds the given column width. Break at word boundaries where possible; break within a word only if the word is longer than the width.

problems.name: Extract all URLs from text
problems.description: Given a block of text, return all HTTP/HTTPS URLs found in it. Each URL should be returned once even if it appears multiple times.

problems.name: Validate email address
problems.description: Return true if the string is a valid email address per RFC 5322 simplified rules: local@domain, local can contain dots/plus/hyphens, domain must have at least one dot.

problems.name: Parse ISO8601 duration string
problems.description: Parse an ISO8601 duration string (e.g. 'P1Y2M3DT4H5M6S') into its component parts and also return the total duration in milliseconds (approximating months as 30 days).

problems.name: Read and parse CSV file
problems.description: Parse a CSV file into an array of objects, using the first row as headers. Handle quoted fields with embedded commas and newlines, and configurable delimiters.

problems.name: Deep set value by dot-path
problems.description: Given an object and a dot-notation path string, set the value at that path, creating intermediate objects/arrays as needed. Return the mutated (or new) object.

problems.name: Invoke Lambda function and parse response
problems.description: Invoke an AWS Lambda function synchronously (RequestResponse). Parse the response payload as JSON. Detect function errors (FunctionError header) and throw with the error detail.

problems.name: Concurrent task runner with concurrency limit
problems.description: Given an array of async tasks (functions returning Promises) and a concurrency limit, run at most N tasks simultaneously. Return results in input order. Propagate all errors.

problems.name: Count lines of code by type
problems.description: Given source code as a string and the language name, count: total lines, code lines, comment lines, and blank lines. Support single-line and multi-line comment styles.

problems.name: Compute content hash for cache key
problems.description: Given an arbitrary JSON-serializable value, produce a stable short hash (e.g. 8-char hex) suitable for use as a cache key. The hash must be identical for deeply equal values regardless of key order.

problems.name: Extract structured data from LLM response
problems.description: Given an LLM response string that may contain a JSON block (inside ```json ... ``` or as bare JSON), extract and parse the JSON. Fall back to a provided default on parse failure.

problems.name: DynamoDB put item with condition
problems.description: Write an item to a DynamoDB table using PutCommand. If the item already exists (attribute_not_exists check), throw a conflict error. Return the written item.

problems.name: Get start and end of a time period
problems.description: Given a Date and a period ('day' | 'week' | 'month' | 'quarter' | 'year'), return { start, end } as Date objects representing the boundaries of that period in the given or local timezone.

problems.name: Paginate through API with cursor
problems.description: Given a fetch function that accepts a cursor and returns { items, next_cursor }, iterate pages until next_cursor is null/undefined. Return all collected items.

problems.name: Generate deterministic UUID from input
problems.description: Given an arbitrary string, generate a UUID v5 (SHA-1 namespace hash) that is deterministic — the same input always produces the same UUID. Accept a namespace UUID or use a default.

problems.name: Omit keys from object
problems.description: Return a new object with the specified keys removed. Deeply nested keys can be specified with dot notation.

problems.name: Deep clone with structured clone or fallback
problems.description: Deep clone a value using structuredClone if available (Node 17+), falling back to JSON round-trip for plain objects. Handle Date, RegExp, Map, and Set correctly.

problems.name: Run shell command with timeout
problems.description: Execute a shell command as a child process. Capture stdout and stderr separately. Kill the process if it exceeds a timeout. Return { stdout, stderr, exitCode, timedOut }.
problems.constraints: timeout in milliseconds; command is a string or string array

problems.name: Flatten nested array to depth N
problems.description: Recursively flatten a nested array up to the given depth. Depth 1 is a shallow flatten; Infinity flattens completely. Return a new array.

problems.name: Publish events to Kinesis stream
problems.description: Given an array of event records, publish them to a Kinesis stream in batches of 500 (the API limit). Use the event ID as the partition key. Handle FailedRecordCount.

problems.name: Topological sort of dependency graph
problems.description: Given a map of node → [dependencies], return a valid topological ordering. Detect and report cycles. Used to order tasks, build steps, or migrations.

problems.name: Diff two JSON objects
problems.description: Given two objects A and B, return a diff describing added keys, removed keys, and changed values. Recurse into nested objects. Output format: { added, removed, changed } with paths.

problems.name: Atomic file write
problems.description: Write content to a file atomically: write to a temp file in the same directory, then rename to the target path. Prevents partial writes from being observed by concurrent readers.

problems.name: Deep get value by dot-path
problems.description: Given an object and a dot-notation path string (e.g. 'a.b.c'), return the value at that path. Return a default if the path does not exist. Supports array index notation (e.g. 'items.0.name').

problems.name: DynamoDB batch write items
problems.description: Write an array of items to DynamoDB in batches of 25 (the API limit). Retry unprocessed items with backoff. Return counts of successful and failed writes.

problems.name: Chunk document for embedding
problems.description: Split a long document into overlapping chunks suitable for embedding and semantic search. Target chunk size in tokens (estimated), with configurable overlap. Return chunks with start/end character offsets.

problems.name: Validate and parse semver string
problems.description: Check if a string is a valid semver (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH with optional pre-release and build metadata). If valid, return the parsed components; if not, return null.

problems.name: Chunk array into pages
problems.description: Given an array and a page size, return an array of sub-arrays where each sub-array has at most pageSize elements. The last chunk may be smaller.

problems.name: Format duration as human-readable string
problems.description: Given a duration in milliseconds, return a human-readable string like '2h 34m 5s' or '450ms'. Omit zero-value units. Support compact and verbose modes.

problems.name: Parse URL into components
problems.description: Given a URL string, return an object with protocol, host, port, pathname, search params (as a map), and hash. Handle relative URLs by resolving against an optional base.

problems.name: SHA-256 hash with optional HMAC
problems.description: Hash a string or Buffer with SHA-256. When a secret key is provided, compute HMAC-SHA256 instead. Return the result as hex, base64, or a Buffer.

problems.name: Parse .env file
problems.description: Read a .env file and return a map of key→value pairs. Handle quoted values, multi-line values (with backslash continuation), inline comments, and export prefixes.

problems.name: Validate UUID
problems.description: Return true if the string is a valid UUID in the standard 8-4-4-4-12 hex format. Optionally validate that it matches a specific version (v1, v4, v5).

problems.name: Build shell command string safely
problems.description: Given a command name and an array of arguments, construct a shell-escaped command string safe for display and logging. Never use this output in eval — use it for logging only.

problems.name: Slugify string for URL
problems.description: Convert an arbitrary string into a URL-safe slug: lowercase, replace spaces and special characters with hyphens, collapse multiple hyphens, strip leading/trailing hyphens.

problems.name: Cosine similarity between two vectors
problems.description: Compute the cosine similarity between two numeric arrays of equal length. Used for embedding-based semantic search. Return a value in [-1, 1]. Handle zero-vectors without division by zero.

problems.name: Template string interpolation
problems.description: Given a template string with {{variable}} placeholders and a data object, return the string with each placeholder replaced by the corresponding value. Missing keys should be left as-is or replaced with a default.

problems.name: Parse key=value string
problems.description: Parse a string of key=value pairs (optionally quoted values, shell-style) into an object. Handle spaces around =, quoted strings with escape sequences, and comment lines starting with #.

problems.name: camelCase to snake_case
problems.description: Convert a camelCase or PascalCase identifier to snake_case. Handles consecutive capitals (e.g. 'XMLParser' → 'xml_parser') and numeric boundaries.

problems.name: Extract import paths from TypeScript file
problems.description: Given TypeScript source code as a string, extract all import paths (from 'import X from ...' and 'import(...)' expressions). Return paths as an array, deduplicated.

problems.name: Compute file hash
problems.description: Compute the MD5 or SHA-256 hash of a file's contents given its path. Stream the file rather than reading it all into memory. Return the hash as a hex string.

problems.name: Truncate string with ellipsis
problems.description: Given a string and a max length, return the string truncated to that length with '...' appended if it was longer. The ellipsis counts toward the max length.

problems.name: Find TODO and FIXME comments
problems.description: Scan source code for TODO, FIXME, HACK, and NOTE comments. Return each match with the line number, comment text, and any ticket reference found inline (e.g. 'TODO(#123)').

problems.name: Deduplicate array preserving order
problems.description: Remove duplicate values from an array, keeping the first occurrence. Support a key extractor function for deduplicating arrays of objects.

problems.name: Parse relative time expression
problems.description: Parse strings like '2 hours ago', 'in 3 days', 'yesterday', 'last week' and return an absolute Date. Return null if the expression is not recognized.

problems.name: Build URL from base and params
problems.description: Given a base URL and an object of query parameters, return the full URL with parameters appended and properly encoded. Undefined/null values are omitted.

problems.name: Retry with exponential backoff
problems.description: Wrap an async function with retry logic: exponential backoff with jitter, configurable max attempts, and a predicate to decide which errors are retryable.

problems.name: Convert date between timezones
problems.description: Given a Date object and a target IANA timezone string (e.g. 'America/New_York'), return the date components (year, month, day, hour, minute, second) as they would appear in that timezone.

problems.name: Extract all values for a key
problems.description: Recursively walk an arbitrarily nested object/array structure and collect every value whose key matches a given name. Return an array of all found values.

problems.name: Debounce and throttle function calls
problems.description: Debounce: delay execution until N ms after the last call. Throttle: allow at most one call per N ms. Both should handle async functions and support a cancel method.

problems.name: CloudWatch put custom metric
problems.description: Publish a custom CloudWatch metric with a namespace, metric name, value, unit, and optional dimensions. Batch multiple metrics into a single PutMetricData call.

problems.name: Redact sensitive fields from log object
problems.description: Given a log object and a list of sensitive field names (e.g. ['password', 'token', 'secret']), return a new object with those field values replaced by '[REDACTED]'. Recurse into nested objects and arrays.

problems.name: Validate IPv4 and IPv6 addresses
problems.description: Given a string, determine if it is a valid IPv4 address (dotted decimal), a valid IPv6 address (including compressed forms), or neither. Return the detected type.

problems.name: List files matching glob patterns
problems.description: Given a root directory and one or more glob patterns, return all matching file paths relative to the root. Support negation patterns. Return paths sorted.

problems.name: HTTP response to normalized error
problems.description: Given an HTTP Response object, detect non-2xx status codes and return a normalized Error with status, code, and message extracted from common error response shapes (JSON { error, message }, { code, detail }, etc.).

problems.name: Sanitize and validate file path
problems.description: Given an untrusted file path string, ensure it doesn't contain path traversal sequences (../) and is within an allowed root directory. Return the resolved absolute path or throw.

problems.name: Truncate prompt to fit token budget
problems.description: Given a prompt string and a max token count, truncate the prompt to fit within the budget. Prefer truncating at sentence or paragraph boundaries. Append a truncation notice if truncated.

problems.name: Compute running statistics over array
problems.description: Given a numeric array, return { min, max, mean, median, stddev, p50, p95, p99 }. Efficient single-pass where possible.

problems.name: Sliding window over array
problems.description: Given an array and a window size, return an array of overlapping sub-arrays of that size. Step size is configurable (default 1). Discard incomplete trailing windows unless padded.

problems.name: Transform array of objects to keyed map
problems.description: Given an array of objects and a key field name, return an object keyed by the value of that field. Handles duplicate keys with a configurable strategy (last-wins, first-wins, array-collect).

problems.name: Token count estimator for LLM context
problems.description: Given a string, estimate the number of LLM tokens it contains using the 4-chars-per-token heuristic, then refine with word-boundary splitting. Return an estimate without requiring a tokenizer library.

problems.name: Diff two arrays as changesets
problems.description: Given arrays A and B, return { added, removed, unchanged } — items in B not in A, items in A not in B, and items in both. Support a key extractor for comparing objects.

problems.name: Deep merge objects
problems.description: Recursively merge two or more objects. Later sources override earlier ones for scalar values; arrays at the same path are replaced (not concatenated) unless a merge strategy is specified.

problems.name: SQS send and receive with visibility timeout
problems.description: Send a message to an SQS queue. Receive messages with a configurable visibility timeout and max count. Delete a message by receipt handle after processing.

problems.name: Validate environment variables against schema
problems.description: Given process.env and a schema (object mapping var names to { type, required, default }), return { valid, missing, invalid, resolved } where resolved contains coerced values.

problems.name: Get secret from Secrets Manager with cache
problems.description: Retrieve a secret value from AWS Secrets Manager. Cache the result in memory for the Lambda instance lifetime. Parse JSON secrets automatically. Support rotation by re-fetching on a configurable TTL.

problems.name: snake_case to camelCase
problems.description: Convert a snake_case or kebab-case identifier to camelCase. Optionally return PascalCase when a flag is set.

problems.name: Calculate business days between dates
problems.description: Count the number of business days (Mon–Fri, excluding provided holidays) between two dates. The start date is inclusive, the end date is exclusive.

problems.name: DynamoDB query GSI with pagination
problems.description: Query a DynamoDB Global Secondary Index by partition key and optional sort key condition. Collect all pages automatically using LastEvaluatedKey. Return all items.

problems.name: Structured JSON logger
problems.description: Build a logger that emits newline-delimited JSON with level, timestamp (ISO8601), message, and arbitrary context fields. Support log levels (debug, info, warn, error) with a configurable minimum level.

problems.name: Strip HTML tags
problems.description: Remove all HTML tags from a string, returning plain text. Preserve text content between tags. Handle self-closing tags and attribute values that contain angle brackets.

problems.name: Redact email addresses in text
problems.description: Replace all email addresses in a string with '[REDACTED]'. Preserve surrounding whitespace and punctuation.

problems.name: Levenshtein distance
problems.description: Compute the edit distance (insertions, deletions, substitutions) between two strings. Used for fuzzy matching and typo detection.

problems.name: Parse package.json and resolve dependency ranges
problems.description: Read package.json from a path, extract all dependencies (dependencies, devDependencies, peerDependencies), and classify each version range as exact, caret, tilde, range, or wildcard.
